data.title: Apple's Week In Review: From Termining Self-Driving Car Permit To Setting iPhone Sales Records
data.description: The tech giant made headlines with its decision to abandon its self-driving car project, the revelation of a secret partnership with Chinese EV manufacturer BYD.

data.title: Cuban Vs Ackman, Dalio's Advice To China, Krugman's Warning, And More: This Week In Economics
data.description: This week saw intense debates between billionaires over political policies, including Mark Cuban and Bill Ackman clashing on Kamala Harris' tax proposal.

data.title: US Offers $10M Reward For Intel On Russian Firm Interfering In Elections
data.description: United States Department of State said earlier this week that it is offering a reward of up to $10 million for information on any foreign person or entity who has engaged or is engaging in interference in the 2024 U.S. presidential election set to be held on Nov. 5.

data.title: Nvidia, Google And Microsoft To Present AI Tools Aimed At Reducing Healthcare Worker Burnout At Las Vegas Conference
data.description: Leading tech firms, including Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA), Alphabet Inc's Google (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG), and Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT), are preparing to showcase their artificial intelligence (AI) tools at the forthcoming HLTH healthcare technology conference in Las Vegas.

data.title: Bitcoin Landfill Lawsuit, Coinbase CEO's Musk Admiration, Anthony Scaramucci's BTC Investment And More: This Week In Crypto
data.description: This past weekend was a whirlwind of activity in the cryptocurrency world. From a man suing a city council over lost Bitcoins (CRYPTO: BTC) to the Coinbase CEO expressing admiration for Elon Musk’s productivity, the stories were as diverse as they were intriguing.
